title: trifolium incarnatum subsp. molinerii
intro: Trifolium incarnatum subsp. molinerii, also known as Red Clover, is a subspecies of clover plant that belongs to the legume family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its isoflavones and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogenic activities. The health benefits of Red Clover include relief from menopausal symptoms, reduced risk of heart disease and osteoporosis, and improved immune function. Red Clover can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support women's health and overall well-being.
